title: Autonomy Kernel Execution State V1
description: The kernel's OWN persisted self-state: mode, cycle bookkeeping, halt status, counters. This is a DIFFERENT artifact from the pre-existing `ops:execution-state` command output (docs/05_operations/schemas/execution-state-v1.md), which reports lane-concurrency state across all active lanes system-wide. Naming collision is intentional to flag, not to imply the two are interchangeable -- see COMPATIBILITY_MAP.md. This file's canonical path (owned by AUT-2, not this lane) must NOT be docs/06_status/lanes/*.json or anything resembling the lane manifest path, to avoid truth-hierarchy ambiguity with LANE_MANIFEST_SPEC.md.

mode.description: Current Mode per STATE_MACHINE.md 2. This is the single field the kill switch's in-band layer sets to halted -- see KILL_SWITCH_CONTRACT.md.

halted.description: Redundant-but-explicit boolean mirror of mode === 'halted', required so the kill switch check (schemas/dispatch_packet_v1.schema.json kill_switch_check) can be a trivial field read rather than a string comparison against a growing enum. mode and halted must never disagree; a writer that would produce disagreement must refuse the write (fail closed) rather than persist an inconsistent state.

halted_reason.description: Required non-null when halted is true. One of: 'owner_kill_switch' | 'auto_halt_consecutive_failures' | 'auto_halt_cost_ceiling' | 'auto_halt_audit_integrity' | 'initial_state' (never promoted yet).

cycle_state.description: Current Cycle state per STATE_MACHINE.md 3. On a fresh process start after a crash, this field's persisted value is NEVER trusted as 'resume from here' -- CRASH_RESTART_SEMANTICS.md 2 requires reconciliation from rank-1/2/3 truth first, after which this field is reset to idle or waking regardless of what it said before the crash.

current_cycle_id.description: Set when cycle_state leaves idle, null when idle. Used as the cycle_id component of every idempotency_key emitted during that cycle.

last_heartbeat_at.description: Updated frequently during an in-progress cycle (at minimum on every cycle-state transition), not just once per cycle. This is the ONLY liveness signal -- see CRASH_RESTART_SEMANTICS.md 1 for why this field, and never a recorded OS PID, is what determines whether a prior invocation is presumed dead.

heartbeat_ttl_seconds.description: TTL applied to last_heartbeat_at for liveness determination. Default per LIMITS.md 5 is 900 (15 minutes) -- generous relative to the expected per-cycle duration cap, so normal cycle jitter never produces a false 'presumed dead'.

owner_pid.description: Informational only -- OS PID of the process that last wrote this record, if known. MUST NOT be used as a liveness signal by any reader (that is exactly the merge-mutex.ts orphaned_pid mistake this system exists to not repeat). Present for human debugging only.

consecutive_infra_failures.description: Consecutive cycles ending in an infra-error class outcome (mirrors ops:truth-check exit code 3 semantics: env/API/manifest unreachable, not a truth failure). Reset to 0 on any cycle that completes gating successfully. Auto-halts the kernel at the LIMITS.md 4 threshold.

consecutive_rollback_triggers.description: Consecutive PROMOTION_ROLLBACK_STANDARDS.md 2 rollback-trigger events. Reset to 0 on any cycle with zero triggers. Distinct counter from consecutive_infra_failures -- a rollback trigger is a mode-safety signal, an infra failure is an environment-health signal.

cost_counters.description: Rolling 24h window per LIMITS.md 6. Reset (window_started_at advanced, counters zeroed) once the window fully elapses -- never reset early by a restart, since restart must reconcile from truth, not from a convenient zero.

active_dispatch_ids.description: idempotency_key values for dispatch packets whose action was started but not yet confirmed complete as of last_heartbeat_at. On restart, every entry here is reconciled (never assumed succeeded or failed) per CRASH_RESTART_SEMANTICS.md 3 before the kernel proceeds to a new cycle.

mode_history.description: Append-only. Never rewritten or truncated. This is the mechanical source for PROGRAM_COMPLETION_DEFINITION.md's promotion/rollback history checks.
